Metadata.homeTitle: Token Forest — Let Your Tokens Grow Into a Tree
Metadata.homeDescription: Token Forest is a desktop pet app: every token you spend with Claude Code or Codex becomes a floating bubble. Click to collect them and grow your pixel tree from seed to fruit-bearing apple tree. Local-first, no telemetry — the optional leaderboard is off by default.
Metadata.dashboardDescription: Your token report card: growth, usage and cost — computed locally, priced offline, never uploaded.

Footer.tagline: Turn your AI token spend into a growing pixel tree.

Hero.treeAlt: Token Forest fully grown pixel apple tree
Hero.tagline: Let your AI tokens grow into a tree
Hero.body: Every token spent in Claude Code / Codex becomes a floating bubble — click to collect and watch your pixel tree slowly grow.
Hero.privacyCopy: 🔒 Local-first · Log-only · No telemetry · Leaderboard off by default

HowItWorks.heading: How It Works
HowItWorks.step01Body: Every time you write code with Claude Code or Codex, each token spent spawns a floating numbered bubble on your desktop.
HowItWorks.step02Body: Tap a bubble and your tree absorbs those tokens — with a tiny pop effect and a +NNN float label. Super satisfying.
HowItWorks.step03Body: Accumulated tokens drive the tree through 8 growth stages, from a tiny seed to a fruit-laden apple tree. Core features are fully local — no network needed.
HowItWorks.privacyBody: Token Forest only reads your local Claude Code / Codex usage logs. Core features run fully offline — no account, no telemetry, nothing uploaded by default. The optional leaderboard stays off until you enable it and confirm its consent dialog.
HowItWorks.videoPlayLabel: Play the Token Forest demo video (1 minute 49 seconds)
HowItWorks.videoPrivacyNote: Press play and the video loads from YouTube, which may set its own cookies. Nothing is loaded until you do.

Features.heading: Features
Features.realtimeBullet0: Incrementally reads Claude Code & Codex usage logs
Features.realtimeBullet1: Bubbles appear the instant tokens are generated — zero lag
Features.growthBullet0: 8 pixel sprite stages, from seed to apple tree
Features.growthBullet1: Progress saved locally to garden.json — survives restarts
Features.privacyBullet0: Reads only local usage logs; uploads nothing by default
Features.privacyBullet1: Core features: no account, no network needed
Features.capsuleBullet0: Full tree above the taskbar — one click shrinks it into a tiny corner capsule
Features.capsuleBullet1: The glow ring is the status: Claude orange · Codex blue · half/half when both run — a shake when done
Features.fruitsBody: Once your tree matures it grows fruit, ripened by token count and time. Harvest them, save them, trade them for cosmetics.
Features.shopBody: Spend fruit on pots, fairy lights, hats, fences, and more — layered onto your pixel tree to make it uniquely yours.
Features.seasonsBody: Auto-switches morning / dusk / night by system time, and spring / summer / autumn / winter by month — falling leaves, snow, blossoms.
Features.lbBody: Available now — opt in from the desktop app. It syncs only the fields shown in the consent dialog, and you compare trees with players worldwide.
Features.dashBullet0: The tree is just the surface — right-click to open its ledger
Features.dashBullet1: Three views, three modes: growth profile · usage stats · chat receipts
Features.dashBullet2: Fully offline pricing: this tree ≈ $128.40 so far

DashboardPage.heading: Your token report card
DashboardPage.tagline: Other tools print a table. Token Forest grows a tree — and keeps the receipts.
DashboardPage.sub: Growth, usage and cost analytics for Claude Code & Codex — computed locally, priced offline, never uploaded.
DashboardPage.mockCaption: UI shown is a demo · numbers are fictional examples · hover to pause
DashboardPage.mockChat2: Add an install section to the README…
DashboardPage.mockChat3: Add tests for leaderboard paging edge cases…
DashboardPage.layersSub: Three views, one set of numbers — tree, charts and receipts always agree.
DashboardPage.layerGrowthBody: A profile for every tree: stage, days planted, growth curve — and what it's worth so far.
DashboardPage.layerUsageBody: Full local analytics: daily, weekly and monthly charts, per-model splits for Claude & Codex, a 26-week heatmap, burn rate, per-project breakdown.
DashboardPage.layerChatsBody: One line per conversation: title, project, prompts, all four token types, estimated cost.
DashboardPage.why0Body: Your stats grow on a tree — numbers you raised, not rows you scroll.
DashboardPage.why1Body: One line per conversation: what it was, which project, what it cost.
DashboardPage.why2Body: The official price table ships inside the app. Zero network at runtime — not an option, the only mode.
DashboardPage.why3Body: Four token types priced separately, cache reads at 0.1× with the math shown — the same accounting the official quotas use.
DashboardPage.faqSub: How the numbers are computed — up front.
DashboardPage.faq0Q: Why are my cache reads in the billions?
DashboardPage.faq0A: That's how agents work: every tool call re-reads the conversation prefix. It's also the cheapest type (~0.1× input) — the dashboard prices it at 0.1× and lists it separately.
DashboardPage.faq1A: They're estimates: local usage × a bundled official price table (with its update date). Not a bill; subscription discounts not included.
DashboardPage.faq2Q: Does any data leave my machine?
DashboardPage.faq2A: By default, no. The dashboard uses metadata only — token counts, model names, timestamps, session titles — never conversation content, all shown locally. The optional leaderboard (off by default) syncs only the fields listed in its consent dialog: an anonymous ID, display name, region, per-tree token totals and growth stages, your app version, and four anti-cheat numbers describing how a score increase was earned — how many 5-minute windows it spans, the busiest one, their sum, and their time span. The per-window timeline itself is never uploaded: those four aggregates cannot reconstruct when you work or rest, and the precise windows never leave your machine.
DashboardPage.faq3Q: How far back does history go?
DashboardPage.faq3A: First open re-parses all local logs (even from before you installed), then a local ledger takes over — your history survives the CLI's 30-day log cleanup.
DashboardPage.faqNote0: Uses only metadata — token counts, model names, timestamps, session titles. Conversation content is never stored or uploaded.
DashboardPage.faqNote1: Costs are estimates from a bundled price table, not a bill.
DashboardPage.how0Body: Install and a tree is planted — it starts growing with every token you spend.
DashboardPage.how1Body: Keep using Claude Code / Codex. Nothing to configure, nothing to change.
DashboardPage.how2Body: Opens in its own resizable window — growth, usage and conversations, any time.
DashboardPage.ctaSub: Free for Windows and macOS. Download and start growing today.
DashboardPage.ctaGithubNote: Head to <link>GitHub</link> and drop a star to catch every new release.

Roadmap.f1Desc: The holiday tree has taken root — and another species is already sprouting in our greenhouse.
Roadmap.f2Desc: The first version is live in the app — and still growing. See the Dashboard page.

DownloadCta.heading: Start Growing Your Tree
DownloadCta.body: For Windows & macOS, free to use. Download and plant yours today.

LeaderboardPage.title: Leaderboard
LeaderboardPage.subtitle: Grow the mightiest tree with the tokens you spend
LeaderboardPage.emptyBody: Download the app and be the first to plant a tree
LeaderboardPage.howTo01Body: Get the Windows / macOS build from the download page and run it.
LeaderboardPage.howTo02Body: Right-click the tree, open Leaderboard → On, and review the consent dialog — it lists exactly what will be synced — then confirm.
LeaderboardPage.howTo03Body: Set your display name in Player Info (blank = anonymous). Your ranking appears on the next sync — what syncs is exactly what the consent dialog lists, nothing more.

DownloadPage.heroTreeAlt: Fully grown Token Forest apple tree
DownloadPage.heroBody: Every token you spend in Claude Code / Codex becomes a floating bubble on your desktop — click to collect and watch your pixel tree grow, blossom, and bear fruit.
DownloadPage.macGuideSummary: 🍎 macOS won't open it? Approve the app — one time (tap for steps)
DownloadPage.macGuideIntro: These builds aren't code-signed yet, so macOS asks you to approve the app on first launch. It's safe, and you only do it once:
DownloadPage.macGuideStep1: Open the .dmg and drag TokenForest.app onto the Applications folder.
DownloadPage.macGuideStep2: Open it from Applications — a "could not verify" dialog appears, then click Done (do NOT click Move to Bin).
DownloadPage.macGuideStep3: Open System Settings → Privacy & Security and scroll down to the Security section:
DownloadPage.macGuideStep4: Click Open Anyway next to the "TokenForest was blocked" message, authenticate, then confirm Open Anyway once more:
DownloadPage.macGuideImg1Alt: Privacy & Security in macOS System Settings
DownloadPage.macGuideImg2Alt: The Open Anyway button in Privacy & Security
DownloadPage.winGuideNote: Windows: fully extract the ZIP first (right-click → Extract All), then run TokenForest.exe from the extracted folder. If SmartScreen appears, click More info → Run anyway.
DownloadPage.petBody: A tiny plot of land hovering above your taskbar, home to a pixel tree that actually grows.
DownloadPage.visualBody: Every token Claude Code / Codex spends appears as a numbered bubble on your desktop.
DownloadPage.privacyBody: Reads local usage logs only. No account, no telemetry — the optional leaderboard is off by default.
DownloadPage.step01Body: Click a button above, or grab the build from <link>GitHub Releases</link>.
DownloadPage.step02Body: Windows: fully extract the ZIP, then run TokenForest.exe. macOS: open the DMG and drag the app into Applications. No command line, no extra dependencies.
DownloadPage.step03Body: Open Token Forest and the sapling appears above your taskbar. Code as usual and watch tokens float up as bubbles.
DownloadPage.sysReq1: Claude Code or OpenAI Codex CLI used on this machine (usage logs present)
DownloadPage.sysReq2: No account needed — core features run fully local
DownloadPage.sysReq3: Lightweight native widget — fast start, low footprint
DownloadPage.githubBody: Questions or ideas? Open an issue on GitHub — and star the repo to catch every new release.
DownloadPage.betaBody: These beta builds aren't code-signed yet: if Windows SmartScreen appears, click More info → Run anyway; on macOS approve the first launch under System Settings → Privacy & Security → Open Anyway.
